clusters.rationale: Both refer to the same phenomenon of involuntary removal by extraterrestrial beings; alien-abduction has 1687 reports vs 58 and is the more common name.
Rename-only: overly clinical agricultural jargon; 'Crop Circles' is the standard term.
All refer to the classic saucer/disc-shaped UFO; disc-ufo has highest count (1285) but flying-saucer is the iconic name; flat-disc and disk-with-dome are variants of the same archetype.
Both describe two spheres/discs connected by a bridge; barbell-ufo has 23 reports vs dumbbell-ufo's 23, same count but barbell is more common terminology.

All describe large triangular silent craft; black-triangle-ufo (315) and silent-triangle (351) are the same phenomenon, arrowhead-ufo is a variant; silent-triangle has highest count but black-triangle is more established.
Both describe V-shaped or boomerang-shaped large triangular craft; chevron-ufo (180) and boomerang-ufo (68) refer to the same sighting type.
All describe elongated cylindrical craft; cigar-ufo (499) is the most common name, cylindrical-ufo (299) and oblong-ufo (56) are variants of the same morphology.

clusters.canonical_name: Close Encounter of the Fourth Kind
clusters.rationale: CE4 explicitly refers to abduction incidents; this is a duplicate of alien-abduction under the Hynek classification system, but alien-abduction is the more direct term and has higher count.
Rename-only: 'Oval UFO' or 'Elliptical UFO' would be clearer; egg-ufo is acceptable but oval-ufo is more standard.
Both describe luminous spherical objects; orb-ufo (2613) has far higher count and is the more standard term.
Tall-grey-alien is a subtype of grey-alien; zeta-reticulan is a specific origin claim for greys but refers to the same entity type; grey-alien (640) is the canonical category.
Both describe coordinated groups of luminous objects in formation; light-formation-ufo (893) and multi-light-formation (754) are the same phenomenon.
Fireball-ufo (428) is a subset of nocturnal-light (5845); nocturnal-light is the broader category and has far higher count.
Both describe geometric box-like craft; rectangle-ufo (326) and cube-ufo (101) are closely related morphologies but distinct enough to keep separate—cubes are 3D boxes, rectangles are 2D profiles.
All describe spherical craft; sphere-ufo (955), orb-ufo (2613), glowing-orb (197), and metallic-sphere-ufo (114) are variants; orb-ufo has highest count and is most common.
All describe three-sided craft; triangular-ufo (1291) is the canonical term; pyramid-ufo (28) and wing-ufo (24) are variants or subsets.
Both refer to the same archetypal small green-skinned humanoid; green-alien (24) and little-green-men (9) describe the same entity type.
Both describe elongated rod-like objects; rod-skyfish (27) and rod-shaped-ufo (16) refer to the same phenomenon.

clusters.canonical_name: Close Encounter of the First Kind
clusters.rationale: CE1 is visual sighting at close range; daylight-disc (531) is a specific subset of CE1 sightings, not a duplicate of the broader classification.

rename_only.rationale: Overly clinical and jargon-heavy; 'Crop Circles' is the standard colloquial term.
Overly clinical/academic phrasing; 'Consciousness-UFO Connection' is more accessible.
Overly clinical; 'EM Effects' or 'Electromagnetic Interference' is how witnesses and researchers refer to this.
Generic forensic terminology; 'UFO Landing Traces' or 'Landing Marks' is more specific to the phenomenon.
Overly technical jargon; 'Water-to-Air UFO' or 'Aquatic UFO' is more intuitive.
Jargon-heavy neologism; 'Subterranean UFO Theory' or 'Underground Origin Theory' is clearer.
